message.content: two issues, this page https://taskai.cc/app/projects/13?tab=wiki&page=54 was created by LLM should show same as comments, Claude Code via Anshuman Biswas (get the exact format, we use this when comments come from LLM), also, look at the tables, they are not being rendered properly https://taskai.cc/app/projects/13?tab=wiki&page=54, do we need to use go-wiki?

content.text: Let me investigate both issues by looking at the relevant code.

input.prompt:
Find how wiki pages display their author/creator in the frontend. Look at:
1. The wiki page component that renders a wiki page (likely in web/src/routes/ or web/src/components/)
2. How comments display "Claude Code via Anshuman Biswas" format - find the exact component and format string
3. The wiki page API response - what fields are returned (check api/internal/api/ for wiki handlers)
4. Check if wiki pages have any LLM/AI attribution fields in the database schema

Be thorough - check both the frontend rendering and the backend API/DB layer.

1→import { useCallback, useEffect, useState } from 'react' 2→import { useSearchParams } from 'react-router-dom' 3→import { api, WikiPage, WikiAnnotation, AnnotationColor, AnnotationComment } from '../lib/api' 4→import WikiEditor from './WikiEditor' 5→import WikiAnnotationSidebar from './WikiAnnotationSidebar' 6→ 7→interface WikiContentProps { 8→ projectId: string 9→} 10→ 11→export default function WikiContent({ projectId }: WikiContentProps) { 12→ const [searchParams, setSearchParams] = useSearchParams() 13→ const selectedPageId = searchParams.get('page') 14→ const annotationParam = searchParams.get('annotation') 15→ const highlightTerm = searchParams.get('highlight') 16→ 17→ const [pages, setPages] = useState([]) 18→ const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true) 19→ const [error, setError] = useState(null) 20→ const [creating, setCreating] = useState(false) 21→ const [newPageTitle, setNewPageTitle] = useState('') 22→ const [showNewPageInput, setShowNewPageInput] = useState(false) 23→ const [searchQuery, setSearchQuery] = useState('') 24→ 25→ const [annotations, setAnnotations] = useState([]) 26→ const [selectedAnnotationId, setSelectedAnnotationId] = useState(null) 27→ const [showAnnotationSidebar, setShowAnnotationSidebar] = useState(false) 28→ const [pinnedAnnotations, setPinnedAnnotations] = useState(false) 29→ const [showResolved, setShowResolved] = useState(false) 30→ 31→ useEffect(() => { 32→ if (projectId) loadPages() 33→ }, [projectId]) // eslint-disable-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps 34→ 35→ useEffect(() => { 36→ if (selectedPageId) { 37→ api.listWikiAnnotations(Number(selectedPageId)) 38→ .then(data => setAnnotations(data)) 39→ .catch(() => setAnnotations([])) 40→ setSelectedAnnotationId(null) 41→ } else { 42→ setAnnotations([]) 43→ } 44→ }, [selectedPageId]) 45→ 46→ // Deep-link to annotation from ?annotation=X (e.g. notification clicks) 47→ useEffect(() => { 48→ if (!annotationParam || !annotations.length) return 49→ const id = Number(annotationParam) 50→ if (!id) return 51→ setSelectedAnnotationId(id) 52→ setShowAnnotationSidebar(true) 53→ }, [annotationParam, annotations]) 54→ 55→ // Highlight search term from ?highlight=X (from command palette wiki search) 56→ useEffect(() => { 57→ if (!highlightTerm || !selectedPageId) return 58→ // Wait for wiki content to render 59→ const timer = setTimeout(() => { 60→ const container = document.querySelector('[data-wiki-content]') || document.querySelector('.ProseMirror') || document.querySelector('.prose') 61→ if (!container) return 62→ const walker = document.createTreeWalker(container, NodeFilter.SHOW_TEXT) 63→ const regex = new RegExp(`(${highlightTerm.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&')})`, 'gi') 64→ const textNodes: Text[] = [] 65→ let node: Node | null 66→ while ((node = walker.nextNode())) { 67→ const text = node.textContent || '' 68→ if (regex.test(text)) { regex.lastIndex = 0; textNodes.push(node as Text) } 69→ } 70→ // Wrap matches in elements 71→ let firstMark: HTMLElement | null = null 72→ for (const textNode of textNodes) { 73→ const parts = (textNode.textContent || '').split(regex) 74→ const frag = document.createDocumentFragment() 75→ for (const part of parts) { 76→ if (regex.test(part)) { 77→ const mark = document.createElement('mark') 78→ mark.textContent = part 79→ mark.style.cssText = 'background: #facc15; color: #1a1a2e; padding: 1px 2px; border-radius: 2px; transition: background 2s ease;' 80→ frag.appendChild(mark) 81→ if (!firstMark) firstMark = mark 82→ regex.lastIndex = 0 83→ } else { 84→ frag.appendChild(document.createTextNode(part)) 85→ } 86→ } 87→ textNode.parentNode?.replaceChild(frag, textNode) 88→ } 89→ // Scroll first match into view 90→ if (firstMark) firstMark.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'center' }) 91→ // Fade out highlights after 4 seconds 92→ setTimeout(() => { 93→ container.querySelectorAll('mark').forEach((m: Element) => { 94→ (m as HTMLElement).style.background = 'transparent' 95→ }) 96→ }, 4000) 97→ // Remove highlight param from URL 98→ const newParams = new URLSearchParams(searchParams) 99→ newParams.delete('highlight') 100→ setSearchParams(newParams, { replace: true }) 101→ }, 800) 102→ return () => clearTimeout(timer) 103→ }, [highlightTerm, selectedPageId]) // eslint-disable-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps 104→ 105→ const loadPages = async () => { 106→ try { 107→ setLoading(true) 108→ setError(null) 109→ const pagesData = await api.getWikiPages(Number(projectId)) 110→ setPages(pagesData.sort((a, b) => b.updated_at.localeCompare(a.updated_at))) 111→ } catch (err) { 112→ setError(err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Failed to load wiki pages') 113→ } finally { 114→ setLoading(false) 115→ } 116→ } 117→ 118→ const handleCreatePage = async () => { 119→ if (!newPageTitle.trim() || !projectId) return 120→ try { 121→ setCreating(true) 122→ const newPage = await api.createWikiPage(Number(projectId),     26→	ID          int64     `json:"id"`

    49→// HandleListWikiPages returns all wiki pages for a project
    50→func (s *Server) HandleListWikiPages(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    51→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(r.Context(), 5*time.Second)
    52→	defer cancel()
    53→
    54→	userID := r.Context().Value(UserIDKey).(int64)
    55→	projectID, err := strconv.ParseInt(chi.URLParam(r, "projectId"), 10, 64)
    56→	if err != nil {
    57→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid project ID", "invalid_input")
    58→		return
    59→	}
    60→
    61→	// Verify user has access to this project
    62→	hasAccess, err := s.checkProjectAccess(ctx, userID, projectID)
    63→	if err != nil {
    64→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to verify project access", "internal_error")
    65→		return
    66→	}
    67→	if !hasAccess {
    68→		respondError(w, http.StatusForbidden, "access denied", "forbidden")
    69→		return
    70→	}
    71→
    72→	// Fetch all wiki pages for the project
    73→	pages, err := s.db.Client.WikiPage.Query().
    74→		Where(wikipage.ProjectID(projectID)).
    75→		WithCreator().
    76→		WithUpdater().
    77→		Order(ent.Asc(wikipage.FieldTitle)).
    78→		All(ctx)
    79→	if err != nil {
    80→		s.logger.Error("Failed to fetch wiki pages",
    81→			zap.Int64("project_id", projectID),
    82→			zap.Error(err),
    83→		)
    84→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to fetch wiki pages", "internal_error")
    85→		return
    86→	}
    87→
    88→	// Convert to response format
    89→	response := make([]WikiPageResponse, 0, len(pages))
    90→	for _, p := range pages {
    91→		wp := WikiPageResponse{
    92→			ID:        p.ID,
    93→			ProjectID: p.ProjectID,
    94→			Title:     p.Title,
    95→			Slug:      p.Slug,
    96→			CreatedBy: p.CreatedBy,
    97→			UpdatedBy: p.UpdatedBy,
    98→			CreatedAt: p.CreatedAt,
    99→			UpdatedAt: p.UpdatedAt,
   100→		}
   101→		if p.Edges.Creator != nil && p.Edges.Creator.Name != nil {
   102→			wp.CreatorName = p.Edges.Creator.Name
   103→		}
   104→		if p.Edges.Updater != nil && p.Edges.Updater.Name != nil {
   105→			wp.UpdaterName = p.Edges.Updater.Name
   106→		}
   107→		response = append(response, wp)
   108→	}
   109→
   110→	respondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, response)
   111→}

   113→// generateSlug creates a URL-friendly slug from a title
   114→func generateSlug(title string) string {
   115→	// Convert to lowercase
   116→	slug := strings.ToLower(title)
   117→	// Replace spaces and special chars with hyphens
   118→	reg := regexp.MustCompile("[^a-z0-9]+")
   119→	slug = reg.ReplaceAllString(slug, "-")
   120→	// Remove leading/trailing hyphens
   121→	slug = strings.Trim(slug, "-")
   122→	// Truncate to max 100 chars
   123→	if len(slug) > 100 {
   124→		slug = slug[:100]
   125→	}
   126→	return slug
   127→}

   129→// HandleCreateWikiPage creates a new wiki page
   130→func (s *Server) HandleCreateWikiPage(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   131→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(r.Context(), 5*time.Second)
   132→	defer cancel()
   133→
   134→	userID := r.Context().Value(UserIDKey).(int64)
   135→	projectID, err := strconv.ParseInt(chi.URLParam(r, "projectId"), 10, 64)
   136→	if err != nil {
   137→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid project ID", "invalid_input")
   138→		return
   139→	}
   140→
   141→	// Verify user has access to this project
   142→	hasAccess, err := s.checkProjectAccess(ctx, userID, projectID)
   143→	if err != nil {
   144→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to verify project access", "internal_error")
   145→		return
   146→	}
   147→	if !hasAccess {
   148→		respondError(w, http.StatusForbidden, "access denied", "forbidden")
   149→		return
   150→	}
   151→
   152→	var req CreateWikiPageRequest
   153→	if err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {
   154→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, errInvalidRequestBody, "invalid_input")
   155→		return
   156→	}
   157→
   158→	// Validation
   159→	if req.Title == "" {
   160→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "title is required", "invalid_input")
   161→		return
   162→	}
   163→	if len(req.Title) > 500 {
   164→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "title is too long (max 500 characters)", "invalid_input")
   165→		return
   166→	}
   167→
   168→	// Generate slug
   169→	baseSlug := generateSlug(req.Title)
   170→	slug := baseSlug
   171→
   172→	// Ensure slug is unique within the project
   173→	for i := 1; i < 100; i++ {
   174→		exists, err := s.db.Client.WikiPage.Query().
   175→			Where(
   176→				wikipage.ProjectID(projectID),
   177→				wikipage.Slug(slug),
   178→			).
   179→			Exist(ctx)
   180→		if err != nil {
   181→			respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to check slug uniqueness", "internal_error")
   182→			return
   183→		}
   184→		if !exists {
   185→			break
   186→		}
   187→		slug = baseSlug + "-" + strconv.Itoa(i)
   188→	}
   189→
   190→	// Create wiki page
   191→	page, err := s.db.Client.WikiPage.Create().
   192→		SetProjectID(projectID).
   193→		SetTitle(req.Title).
   194→		SetSlug(slug).
   195→		SetCreatedBy(userID).
   196→		Save(ctx)
   197→	if err != nil {
   198→		s.logger.Error("Failed to create wiki page",
   199→			zap.Int64("project_id", projectID),
   200→			zap.String("title", req.Title),
   201→			zap.Error(err),
   202→		)
   203→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to create wiki page", "internal_error")
   204→		return
   205→	}
   206→
   207→	response := WikiPageResponse{
   208→		ID:        page.ID,
   209→		ProjectID: page.ProjectID,
   210→		Title:     page.Title,
   211→		Slug:      page.Slug,
   212→		CreatedBy: page.CreatedBy,
   213→		CreatedAt: page.CreatedAt,
   214→		UpdatedAt: page.UpdatedAt,
   215→	}
   216→
   217→	respondJSON(w, http.StatusCreated, response)
   218→}

   220→// HandleGetWikiPage returns a single wiki page
   221→func (s *Server) HandleGetWikiPage(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   222→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(r.Context(), 5*time.Second)

   238→		Only(ctx)
   239→	if err != nil {

   244→		s.logger.Error("Failed to fetch wiki page",

   252→	// Verify user has access to the project
   253→	hasAccess, err := s.checkProjectAccess(ctx, userID, page.ProjectID)
   254→	if err != nil {
   255→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to verify project access", "internal_error")
   256→		return
   257→	}
   258→	if !hasAccess {

   270→		Content:   &page.Content,
   271→		CreatedAt: page.CreatedAt,
   272→		UpdatedAt: page.UpdatedAt,
   273→	}
   274→	if page.Edges.Creator != nil && page.Edges.Creator.Name != nil {
   275→		response.CreatorName = page.Edges.Creator.Name
   276→	}
   277→	if page.Edges.Updater != nil && page.Edges.Updater.Name != nil {
   278→		response.UpdaterName = page.Edges.Updater.Name
   279→	}
   280→
   281→	respondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, response)
   282→}

   284→// HandleUpdateWikiPage updates a wiki page
   285→func (s *Server) HandleUpdateWikiPage(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   286→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(r.Context(), 5*time.Second)
   287→	defer cancel()
   288→
   289→	userID := r.Context().Value(UserIDKey).(int64)
   290→	pageID, err := strconv.ParseInt(chi.URLParam(r, "pageId"), 10, 64)
   291→	if err != nil {
   292→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid page ID", "invalid_input")
   293→		return
   294→	}
   295→
   296→	// Fetch the wiki page
   297→	page, err := s.db.Client.WikiPage.Query().
   298→		Where(wikipage.ID(pageID)).
   299→		Only(ctx)
   300→	if err != nil {
   301→		if ent.IsNotFound(err) {
   302→			respondError(w, http.StatusNotFound, "wiki page not found", "not_found")
   303→			return
   304→		}
   305→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to fetch wiki page", "internal_error")
   306→		return
   307→	}
   308→
   309→	// Verify user has access to the project
   310→	hasAccess, err := s.checkProjectAccess(ctx, userID, page.ProjectID)
   311→	if err != nil {
   312→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to verify project access", "internal_error")
   313→		return
   314→	}
   315→	if !hasAccess {
   316→		respondError(w, http.StatusForbidden, "access denied", "forbidden")
   317→		return
   318→	}
   319→
   320→	var req UpdateWikiPageRequest
   321→	if err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {
   322→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, errInvalidRequestBody, "invalid_input")
   323→		return
   324→	}
   325→
   326→	// Update wiki page
   327→	update := s.db.Client.WikiPage.UpdateOneID(pageID)
   328→
   329→	if req.Title != nil {
   330→		if *req.Title == "" {
   331→			respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "title cannot be empty", "invalid_input")
   332→			return
   333→		}
   334→		if len(*req.Title) > 500 {
   335→			respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "title is too long (max 500 characters)", "invalid_input")
   336→			return
   337→		}
   338→		update.SetTitle(*req.Title)
   339→
   340→		// Regenerate slug if title changed
   341→		if *req.Title != page.Title {
   342→			newSlug := generateSlug(*req.Title)
   343→			update.SetSlug(newSlug)
   344→		}
   345→	}
   346→
   347→	updatedPage, err := update.Save(ctx)
   348→	if err != nil {
   349→		s.logger.Error("Failed to update wiki page",
   350→			zap.Int64("page_id", pageID),
   351→			zap.Error(err),
   352→		)
   353→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to update wiki page", "internal_error")
   354→		return
   355→	}
   356→
   357→	response := WikiPageResponse{
   358→		ID:        updatedPage.ID,
   359→		ProjectID: updatedPage.ProjectID,
   360→		Title:     updatedPage.Title,
   361→		Slug:      updatedPage.Slug,
   362→		CreatedBy: updatedPage.CreatedBy,
   363→		CreatedAt: updatedPage.CreatedAt,
   364→		UpdatedAt: updatedPage.UpdatedAt,
   365→	}
   366→
   367→	respondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, response)
   368→}

   447→// HandleUpdateWikiPageContent updates the content of a wiki page
   448→func (s *Server) HandleUpdateWikiPageContent(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   449→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(r.Context(), 5*time.Second)

   469→		return
   470→	}
   471→
   472→	// Verify user has access to the project
   473→	hasAccess, err := s.checkProjectAccess(ctx, userID, page.ProjectID)
   474→	if err != nil {
   475→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to verify project access", "internal_error")
   476→		return
   477→	}
   478→	if !hasAccess {
   479→		respondError(w, http.StatusForbidden, "access denied", "forbidden")
   480→		return
   481→	}
   482→
   483→	var req UpdateWikiPageContentRequest
   484→	if err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {
   485→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, errInvalidRequestBody, "invalid_input")
   486→		return
   487→	}
   488→
   489→	updatedPage, err := s.db.Client.WikiPage.UpdateOneID(pageID).
   490→		SetContent(req.Content).
   491→		SetUpdatedBy(userID).
   492→		Save(ctx)
   493→	if err != nil {
   494→		s.logger.Error("Failed to update wiki page content",
   495→			zap.Int64("page_id", pageID),
   496→			zap.Error(err),
   497→		)
   498→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to update wiki page content", "internal_error")
   499→		return
   500→	}
   501→
   502→	// Set agent_name via raw SQL if present (wiki_pages Ent schema doesn't have this field yet)
   503→	agentName := GetAgentName(r)

   507→		}

   511→			s.logger.Warn("Failed to clear agent_name on wiki page", zap.Error(err), zap.Int64("page_id", pageID))
   512→		}

   524→
   525→	respondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, WikiPageContentResponse{

   532→// maybeCreateVersion creates a version snapshot if versioning criteria are met.
   533→func (s *Server) maybeCreateVersion(ctx context.Context, pageID, userID int64, newContent string, manualSave bool, agentName *string) error {
   534→	hash := fmt.Sprintf("%x", sha256.Sum256([]byte(newContent)))
   535→
   536→	// Fetch last version
   537→	lastVersion, err := s.db.Client.WikiPageVersion.Query().
   538→		Where(wikipageversion.WikiPageID(pageID)).
   539→		Order(ent.Desc(wikipageversion.FieldVersionNumber)).
   540→		First(ctx)
   541→	if err != nil && !ent.IsNotFound(err) {
   542→		return err
   543→	}
   544→
   545→	// Skip if content identical to last version
   546→	if lastVersion != nil && lastVersion.ContentHash == hash {
   547→		return nil
   548→	}
   549→
   550→	shouldVersion := manualSave
   551→	if !shouldVersion {
   552→		if lastVersion == nil {
   553→			// First content — version it
   554→			if newContent != "" {
   555→				shouldVersion = true
   556→			}
   557→		} else if time.Since(lastVersion.CreatedAt) > 15*time.Minute {
   558→			// Time-based snapshot
   559→			shouldVersion = true
   560→		} else if isSignificantChange(lastVersion.Content, newContent) {
   561→			// Large diff
   562→			shouldVersion = true
   563→		}
   564→	}
   565→
   566→	if !shouldVersion {
   567→		return nil
   568→	}
   569→
   570→	versionNum := 1
   571→	if lastVersion != nil {
   572→		versionNum = lastVersion.VersionNumber + 1
   573→	}
   574→
   575→	ver, err := s.db.Client.WikiPageVersion.Create().
   576→		SetWikiPageID(pageID).
   577→		SetVersionNumber(versionNum).
   578→		SetContent(newContent).
   579→		SetContentHash(hash).
   580→		SetCreatedBy(userID).
   581→		Save(ctx)
   582→	if err != nil {
   583→		return err
   584→	}
   585→
   586→	// Set agent_name via raw SQL (Ent schema doesn't have this field yet)
   587→	if agentName != nil {
   588→		if _, execErr := s.db.ExecContext(ctx, `UPDATE wiki_page_versions SET agent_name = $1 WHERE id = $2`, *agentName, ver.ID); execErr != nil {
   589→			s.logger.Warn("Failed to set agent_name on wiki version", zap.Error(execErr), zap.Int64("version_id", ver.ID))
   590→		}
   591→	}
   592→	return nil
   593→}
   594→
   595→// isSignificantChange returns true when the diff is >15% of old or >500 chars changed.
   596→func isSignificantChange(oldContent, newContent string) bool {
   597→	oldLines := strings.Split(oldContent, "\n")
   598→	newLines := strings.Split(newContent, "\n")
   599→
   600→	oldSet := make(map[string]int, len(oldLines))
   601→	for _, l := range oldLines {
   602→		oldSet[l]++
   603→	}
   604→
   605→	charsChanged := 0
   606→	for _, l := range newLines {
   607→		if oldSet[l] > 0 {
   608→			oldSet[l]--
   609→		} else {
   610→			charsChanged += len(l)
   611→		}
   612→	}
   613→	// Also count lines removed from old
   614→	for l, cnt := range oldSet {
   615→		charsChanged += len(l) * cnt
   616→	}
   617→
   618→	if charsChanged > 500 {
   619→		return true
   620→	}
   621→	if len(oldContent) > 0 && float64(charsChanged) > 0.15*float64(len(oldContent)) {
   622→		return true
   623→	}
   624→	return false
   625→}

`, u, size) 49→ }) 50→} 51→ 52→// preprocessGraphLinksForPreview converts [[wiki:ID|Label]] / [[task:ID|Label]] syntax 53→// into styled inline HTML elements before markdown rendering. 54→func preprocessGraphLinksForPreview(content string) string { 55→ return graphLinkPreRe.ReplaceAllStringFunc(content, func(match string) string { 56→ m := graphLinkPreRe.FindStringSubmatch(match) 57→ if len(m) < 3 { 58→ return match 59→ } 60→ entityType, entityID, label := m[1], m[2], m[3] 61→ if label == "" { 62→ if entityType == "wiki" { 63→ label = "Wiki #" + entityID 64→ } else { 65→ label = "Task #" + entityID 66→ } 67→ } 68→ icon := "📄" 69→ baseColor := "#3b82f6" 70→ bgColor := "rgba(59,130,246,0.15)" 71→ borderColor := "rgba(59,130,246,0.3)" 72→ if entityType == "task" { 73→ icon = "✅" 74→ baseColor = "#f97316" 75→ bgColor = "rgba(249,115,22,0.15)" 76→ borderColor = "rgba(249,115,22,0.3)" 77→ } 78→ style := fmt.Sprintf( 79→ "display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:4px;padding:1px 8px;border-radius:9999px;font-size:11px;font-weight:500;background:%s;color:%s;border:1px solid %s;text-decoration:none;cursor:pointer;vertical-align:middle", 80→ bgColor, baseColor, borderColor, 81→ ) 82→ return fmt.Sprintf( 83→ `%s %s`, 84→ entityType, entityID, style, icon, label, 85→ ) 86→ }) 87→} 88→ 89→// stripDrawEditMode removes /edit from go-draw embed URLs so that 90→// rendered content always shows a read-only canvas viewer. 91→func stripDrawEditMode(html string) string { 92→ return drawEditSrcRe.ReplaceAllString(html, `$1"`) 93→} 94→ 95→// wikiPreviewRequest is the JSON body for the preview endpoint. 96→type wikiPreviewRequest struct { 97→ Content string `json:"content"` 98→} 99→ 100→// wikiPreviewResponse is the JSON response from the preview endpoint. 101→type wikiPreviewResponse struct { 102→ HTML string `json:"html"` 103→} 104→ 105→// HandleWikiPreview renders markdown content to HTML using the go-wiki renderer. 106→func (s *Server) HandleWikiPreview(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) { 107→ if r.Method != http.MethodPost { 108→ respondError(w, http.StatusMethodNotAllowed, "method not allowed", "method_not_allowed") 109→ return 110→ } 111→ 112→ body, err := io.ReadAll(r.Body) 113→ if err != nil { 114→ respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "failed to read request body", "invalid_input") 115→ return 116→ } 117→ defer r.Body.Close() 118→ 119→ var req wikiPreviewRequest 120→ if err := json.Unmarshal(body, &req); err != nil { 121→ respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid JSON body", "invalid_input") 122→ return 123→ } 124→ 125→ html := stripDrawEditMode(wiki.RenderContent(preprocessFigmaShortcodes(preprocessGraphLinksForPreview(req.Content)))) 126→ 127→ respondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, wikiPreviewResponse{HTML: html}) 128→} 129→

1→package api
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"encoding/json"
     6→	"errors"
     7→	"net/http"
     8→	"strconv"
     9→	"time"
    10→
    11→	"github.com/go-chi/chi/v5"
    12→	"go.uber.org/zap"
    13→
    14→	"taskai/ent"
    15→)
    16→
    17→// ── Types ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    18→
    19→// WikiAnnotation is a text highlight with threaded comments on a wiki page.
    20→type WikiAnnotation struct {
    21→	ID           int64               `json:"id"`
    22→	WikiPageID   int64               `json:"wiki_page_id"`
    23→	AuthorID     int64               `json:"author_id"`
    24→	AuthorName   *string             `json:"author_name,omitempty"`
    25→	StartOffset  int                 `json:"start_offset"`
    26→	EndOffset    int                 `json:"end_offset"`
    27→	SelectedText string              `json:"selected_text"`
    28→	Color        string              `json:"color"`
    29→	Resolved     bool                `json:"resolved"`
    30→	CreatedAt    time.Time           `json:"created_at"`
    31→	Comments     []AnnotationComment `json:"comments"`
    32→}
    33→
    34→// AnnotationComment is a comment (or reply) on a wiki annotation.
    35→type AnnotationComment struct {
    36→	ID              int64     `json:"id"`
    37→	AnnotationID    int64     `json:"annotation_id"`
    38→	AuthorID        int64     `json:"author_id"`
    39→	AuthorName      *string   `json:"author_name,omitempty"`
    40→	ParentCommentID *int64    `json:"parent_comment_id,omitempty"`
    41→	Content         string    `json:"content"`
    42→	Resolved        bool      `json:"resolved"`
    43→	CreatedAt       time.Time `json:"created_at"`
    44→	UpdatedAt       time.Time `json:"updated_at"`
    45→}
    46→
    47→var errAnnotationForbidden = errors.New("forbidden")
    48→
    49→var validAnnotationColors = map[string]bool{
    50→	"yellow": true,
    51→	"blue":   true,
    52→	"green":  true,
    53→	"red":    true,
    54→}
    55→
    56→// ── Access helper ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    57→
    58→func (s *Server) checkWikiPageAccess(ctx context.Context, userID, pageID int64) error {
    59→	page, err := s.db.Client.WikiPage.Get(ctx, pageID)
    60→	if err != nil {
    61→		return err
    62→	}
    63→	hasAccess, err := s.checkProjectAccess(ctx, userID, page.ProjectID)
    64→	if err != nil {
    65→		return err
    66→	}
    67→	if !hasAccess {
    68→		return errAnnotationForbidden
    69→	}
    70→	return nil
    71→}
    72→
    73→func handleWikiAccessError(w http.ResponseWriter, err error) {
    74→	if ent.IsNotFound(err) {
    75→		respondError(w, http.StatusNotFound, "wiki page not found", "not_found")
    76→	} else if err == errAnnotationForbidden {
    77→		respondError(w, http.StatusForbidden, "access denied", "forbidden")
    78→	} else {
    79→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to verify access", "internal_error")
    80→	}
    81→}
    82→
    83→// ── Annotation handlers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    84→
    85→// HandleListWikiAnnotations returns all annotations for a page, each with its comments.
    86→func (s *Server) HandleListWikiAnnotations(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    87→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(r.Context(), 5*time.Second)
    88→	defer cancel()
    89→
    90→	userID := r.Context().Value(UserIDKey).(int64)
    91→	pageID, err := strconv.ParseInt(chi.URLParam(r, "pageId"), 10, 64)
    92→	if err != nil {
    93→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid page ID", "invalid_input")
    94→		return
    95→	}
    96→
    97→	if err := s.checkWikiPageAccess(ctx, userID, pageID); err != nil {
    98→		handleWikiAccessError(w, err)
    99→		return
   100→	}
   101→
   102→	// Fetch annotations ordered by position
   103→	rows, err := s.db.QueryContext(ctx, `
   104→		SELECT a.id, a.wiki_page_id, a.author_id, u.name,
   105→		       a.start_offset, a.end_offset, a.selected_text, a.color, a.resolved, a.created_at
   106→		FROM wiki_annotations a
   107→		LEFT JOIN users u ON u.id = a.author_id
   108→		WHERE a.wiki_page_id = $1
   109→		ORDER BY a.start_offset ASC
   110→	`, pageID)
   111→	if err != nil {
   112→		s.logger.Error("Failed to fetch wiki annotations", zap.Error(err))
   113→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to fetch annotations", "internal_error")
   114→		return
   115→	}
   116→	defer rows.Close()
   117→
   118→	annotations := []WikiAnnotation{}
   119→	annIndex := map[int64]int{} // annotation ID → index in slice
   120→
   121→	for rows.Next() {
   122→		var a WikiAnnotation
   123→		if scanErr := rows.Scan(
   124→			&a.ID, &a.WikiPageID, &a.AuthorID, &a.AuthorName,
   125→			&a.StartOffset, &a.EndOffset, &a.SelectedText, &a.Color, &a.Resolved, &a.CreatedAt,
   126→		); scanErr != nil {
   127→			continue
   128→		}
   129→		a.Comments = []AnnotationComment{}
   130→		annIndex[a.ID] = len(annotations)
   131→		annotations = append(annotations, a)
   132→	}
   133→	if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
   134→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to read annotations", "internal_error")
   135→		return
   136→	}
   137→
   138→	if len(annotations) > 0 {
   139→		// Fetch all comments for this page's annotations in one query
   140→		commentRows, cErr := s.db.QueryContext(ctx, `
   141→			SELECT c.id, c.annotation_id, c.author_id, u.name, c.parent_comment_id,
   142→			       c.content, c.resolved, c.created_at, c.updated_at
   143→			FROM wiki_annotation_comments c
   144→			LEFT JOIN users u ON u.id = c.author_id
   145→			JOIN wiki_annotations a ON a.id = c.annotation_id
   146→			WHERE a.wiki_page_id = $1
   147→			ORDER BY c.created_at ASC
   148→		`, pageID)
   149→		if cErr == nil {
   150→			defer commentRows.Close()
   151→			for commentRows.Next() {
   152→				var c AnnotationComment
   153→				if scanErr := commentRows.Scan(
   154→					&c.ID, &c.AnnotationID, &c.AuthorID, &c.AuthorName,
   155→					&c.ParentCommentID, &c.Content, &c.Resolved, &c.CreatedAt, &c.UpdatedAt,
   156→				); scanErr != nil {
   157→					continue
   158→				}
   159→				if idx, ok := annIndex[c.AnnotationID]; ok {
   160→					annotations[idx].Comments = append(annotations[idx].Comments, c)
   161→				}
   162→			}
   163→		}
   164→	}
   165→
   166→	respondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, annotations)
   167→}
   168→
   169→// HandleCreateWikiAnnotation creates a new annotation with an optional first comment.
   170→func (s *Server) HandleCreateWikiAnnotation(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   171→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(r.Context(), 5*time.Second)
   172→	defer cancel()
   173→
   174→	userID := r.Context().Value(UserIDKey).(int64)
   175→	pageID, err := strconv.ParseInt(chi.URLParam(r, "pageId"), 10, 64)
   176→	if err != nil {
   177→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid page ID", "invalid_input")
   178→		return
   179→	}
   180→
   181→	if err := s.checkWikiPageAccess(ctx, userID, pageID); err != nil {
   182→		handleWikiAccessError(w, err)
   183→		return
   184→	}
   185→
   186→	var req struct {
   187→		StartOffset  int    `json:"start_offset"`
   188→		EndOffset    int    `json:"end_offset"`
   189→		SelectedText string `json:"selected_text"`
   190→		Color        string `json:"color"`
   191→		Comment      string `json:"comment"`
   192→	}
   193→	if err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {
   194→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, errInvalidRequestBody, "invalid_input")
   195→		return
   196→	}
   197→
   198→	if req.SelectedText == "" {
   199→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "selected_text is required", "invalid_input")
   200→		return
   201→	}
   202→	if req.StartOffset < 0 || req.EndOffset <= req.StartOffset {
   203→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid offsets", "invalid_input")
   204→		return
   205→	}
   206→	if req.Color == "" {
   207→		req.Color = "yellow"
   208→	}
   209→	if !validAnnotationColors[req.Color] {
   210→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid color (yellow/blue/green/red)", "invalid_input")
   211→		return
   212→	}
   213→
   214→	var annotationID int64
   215→	err = s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `
   216→		INSERT INTO wiki_annotations (wiki_page_id, author_id, start_offset, end_offset, selected_text, color)
   217→		VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6)
   218→		RETURNING id
   219→	`, pageID, userID, req.StartOffset, req.EndOffset, req.SelectedText, req.Color).Scan(&annotationID)
   220→	if err != nil {
   221→		s.logger.Error("Failed to create wiki annotation", zap.Error(err))
   222→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to create annotation", "internal_error")
   223→		return
   224→	}
   225→
   226→	// Optionally add first comment
   227→	if req.Comment != "" && len(req.Comment) <= 5000 {
   228→		if _, cErr := s.db.ExecContext(ctx, `
   229→			INSERT INTO wiki_annotation_comments (annotation_id, author_id, content)
   230→			VALUES ($1, $2, $3)
   231→		`, annotationID, userID, req.Comment); cErr != nil {
   232→			s.logger.Warn("Failed to create initial annotation comment", zap.Error(cErr))
   233→		}
   234→	}
   235→
   236→	// Return full annotation with comments
   237→	annotation := s.fetchAnnotationByID(ctx, annotationID)
   238→	if annotation == nil {
   239→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to fetch created annotation", "internal_error")
   240→		return
   241→	}
   242→
   243→	respondJSON(w, http.StatusCreated, annotation)
   244→}
   245→
   246→// HandleDeleteWikiAnnotation deletes an annotation (author only).
   247→func (s *Server) HandleDeleteWikiAnnotation(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   248→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(r.Context(), 5*time.Second)
   249→	defer cancel()
   250→
   251→	userID := r.Context().Value(UserIDKey).(int64)
   252→	annotationID, err := strconv.ParseInt(chi.URLParam(r, "annotationId"), 10, 64)
   253→	if err != nil {
   254→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid annotation ID", "invalid_input")
   255→		return
   256→	}
   257→
   258→	// Verify ownership and access
   259→	var authorID int64
   260→	var pageID int64
   261→	if err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
   262→		`SELECT author_id, wiki_page_id FROM wiki_annotations WHERE id = $1`, annotationID,
   263→	).Scan(&authorID, &pageID); err != nil {
   264→		respondError(w, http.StatusNotFound, "annotation not found", "not_found")
   265→		return
   266→	}
   267→
   268→	if err := s.checkWikiPageAccess(ctx, userID, pageID); err != nil {
   269→		handleWikiAccessError(w, err)
   270→		return
   271→	}
   272→	if authorID != userID {
   273→		respondError(w, http.StatusForbidden, "only the author can delete an annotation", "forbidden")
   274→		return
   275→	}
   276→
   277→	if _, err := s.db.ExecContext(ctx, `DELETE FROM wiki_annotations WHERE id = $1`, annotationID); err != nil {
   278→		s.logger.Error("Failed to delete wiki annotation", zap.Error(err))
   279→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to delete annotation", "internal_error")
   280→		return
   281→	}
   282→
   283→	w.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)
   284→}
   285→
   286→// HandleUpdateWikiAnnotation updates color or resolved state of an annotation.
   287→func (s *Server) HandleUpdateWikiAnnotation(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   288→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(r.Context(), 5*time.Second)

   293→	if err != nil {
   294→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid annotation ID", "invalid_input")
   295→		return
   296→	}

   300→		Resolved *bool   `json:"resolved,omitempty"`

1→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS wiki_annotations (
     2→    id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
     3→    wiki_page_id BIGINT NOT NULL REFERENCES wiki_pages(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
     4→    author_id BIGINT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
     5→    start_offset INTEGER NOT NULL,
     6→    end_offset INTEGER NOT NULL,

content.content:
CREATE TABLE wiki_pages (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    project_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    title TEXT NOT NULL,
    slug TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_by INTEGER NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, content TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '', updated_by INTEGER REFERENCES users(id),
    UNIQUE(project_id, slug),
    FOREIGN KEY (project_id) REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    FOREIGN KEY (created_by) REFERENCES users(id)
);
CREATE INDEX idx_wiki_pages_project_id ON wiki_pages(project_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_wiki_pages_slug ON wiki_pages(slug);

export interface WikiPage {
  id: number
  project_id: number
  title: string
  slug: string
  created_by: number
  creator_name?: string
  updated_by?: number
  updater_name?: string
  created_at: string
  updated_at: string
}

export interface AppNotification {
  id: number
  sender_id?: number | null
  sender_name?: string | null
  type: string
  entity_type: string
  entity_id: number

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a final comprehensive report.

## Summary

Based on my thorough analysis of the TaskAI codebase, here's how wiki pages display their author/creator and how AI/agent attribution works:

### Frontend Display of Wiki Page Author/Creator

**File:** `/Users/anshumanbiswas/play/taskai/web/src/components/WikiEditor.tsx` (lines 1563-1584)

The wiki page displays author information in the header:
- **Created by**: Shows the creator's name with a link to their profile
- **Last edited by**: Shows the updater's name (if different from creator) with a link and timestamp

The format is:
```
Created by [Creator Name] · [creation date]
Last edited by [Updater Name] · [last edit timestamp]
```

### Backend API Response

**File:** `/Users/anshumanbiswas/play/taskai/api/internal/api/wiki_handlers.go` (lines 25-37)

The `WikiPageResponse` struct includes:
```go
type WikiPageResponse struct {
    ID          int64
    ProjectID   int64
    Title       string
    Slug        string
    CreatedBy   int64
    CreatorName *string   // Populated from User.Name
    UpdatedBy   *int64
    UpdaterName *string   // Populated from User.Name
    Content     *string
    CreatedAt   time.Time
    UpdatedAt   time.Time
}
```

### Database Schema

**Files:** 
- `/Users/anshumanbiswas/play/taskai/api/ent/schema/wikipage.go`
- `/Users/anshumanbiswas/play/taskai/api/internal/db/migrations/061_wiki_agent_name.sql`

The `wiki_pages` table has:
- `created_by` (user ID) - references users table
- `updated_by` (nullable user ID) - references users table
- `agent_name` (VARCHAR(100), nullable) - stores AI agent attribution (e.g., "Claude Code")

When fetching wiki pages, the backend performs a LEFT JOIN with the users table to populate `creator_name` and `updater_name`.

### AI/Agent Attribution Pattern

**Files:**
- `/Users/anshumanbiswas/play/taskai/api/internal/api/wiki_handlers.go` (lines 502-513)
- `/Users/anshumanbiswas/play/taskai/api/internal/api/middleware.go` (lines 177-189)

**How it works:**
1. AI agents send an `X-Agent-Name` header (e.g., "Claude Code")
2. The middleware extracts this header and stores it in the request context via `AgentNameKey`
3. When a wiki page is updated, `GetAgentName(r)` retrieves the agent name
4. If present, the agent name is stored in the `wiki_pages.agent_name` column
5. If a human edits, the agent_name is cleared (set to NULL)

### Annotation Comments Display

**File:** `/Users/anshumanbiswas/play/taskai/web/src/components/WikiAnnotationSidebar.tsx` (lines 365-372)

Annotation comments display author information similarly:
```typescript
<Link to={`/app/users/${comment.author_id}`}>
  {comment.author_name ?? 'Unknown'}
</Link>
```

The backend fetches comments with author names via LEFT JOIN on the users table (lines 141-148 of `wiki_annotation_handlers.go`).

### Key Finding: No Current Agent Attribution on Wiki Pages in Frontend

Currently, the wiki pages display only `creator_name` and `updater_name` (the user names). The `agent_name` field exists in the database but is **not yet displayed in the frontend** for wiki pages. 

However, the pattern for agent attribution is already implemented for:
- **Task comments**: Shows format like `"Claude Code for John Doe"` with an "AI" badge (TaskDetail.tsx, lines 1106-1114)

This same pattern could be applied to wiki pages to show: `"Claude Code via John Doe"` or similar format when `agent_name` is present.

Find how wiki pages display their author/creator in the frontend. Look at:
1. The wiki page component that renders a wiki page (likely in web/src/routes/ or web/src/components/)
2. How comments display "Claude Code via Anshuman Biswas" format - find the exact component and format string
3. The wiki page API response - what fields are returned (check api/internal/api/ for wiki handlers)
4. Check if wiki pages have any LLM/AI attribution fields in the database schema

Be thorough - check both the frontend rendering and the backend API/DB layer.

### Annotation Comments Display

**File:** `/Users/anshumanbiswas/play/taskai/web/src/components/WikiAnnotationSidebar.tsx` (lines 365-372)

Annotation comments display author information similarly:
```typescript
<Link to={`/app/users/${comment.author_id}`}>
  {comment.author_name ?? 'Unknown'}
</Link>
```

The backend fetches comments with author names via LEFT JOIN on the users table (lines 141-148 of `wiki_annotation_handlers.go`).

### Key Finding: No Current Agent Attribution on Wiki Pages in Frontend

Currently, the wiki pages display only `creator_name` and `updater_name` (the user names). The `agent_name` field exists in the database but is **not yet displayed in the frontend** for wiki pages. 

However, the pattern for agent attribution is already implemented for:
- **Task comments**: Shows format like `"Claude Code for John Doe"` with an "AI" badge (TaskDetail.tsx, lines 1106-1114)

This same pattern could be applied to wiki pages to show: `"Claude Code via John Doe"` or similar format when `agent_name` is present.

Perfect! Now I have a complete picture. Let me create a comprehensive summary of my findings: ## Summary: Wiki Page Content Rendering for Markdown Tables I've thoroughly analyzed how wiki page content is rendered in the TaskAI frontend, specifically for markdown tables. Here are my findings: ### 1. Frontend Markdown Rendering Component **File:** `/Users/anshumanbiswas/play/taskai/web/src/components/WikiEditor.tsx` - The `PreviewContent` component renders markdown HTML using: - `className="prose prose-invert max-w-none"` - `dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: previewHTML }}` (line 833) - The preview HTML is fetched from the backend via `PREVIEW_ENDPOINT = /api/wiki/preview` - Rendering happens in the `PreviewContent` function (lines 823-856) ### 2. Markdown Renderer Being Used **Backend Package:** `go-wiki` v0.1.3 (`github.com/anchoo2kewl/go-wiki`) **File:** `/Users/anshumanbiswas/play/taskai/api/internal/api/wiki_preview_handler.go` The backend uses the `go-wiki` package which wraps the `blackfriday` v2 markdown renderer. Key details: - **Table Support:** Enabled via `blackfriday.Tables` flag in the `renderMarkdown()` function (line 175 in renderer.go) - **Table Normalization:** Tables are preprocessed via `normalizeInlinePipeTables()` to handle inline pipe tables that were collapsed into single lines (line 119 in renderer.go) - The wiki instance is configured with: ```go wiki = gowiki.New( gowiki.WithClassConfig(render.ClassConfig{ ULClass: "list-disc pl-6 space-y-1", OLClass: "list-decimal pl-6 space-y-1", LIClass: "", BlockquoteClass: "border-l-4 border-blue-500/40 pl-4 italic text-gray-300", }), gowiki.WithDrawBasePath("/draw"), ) ``` ### 3. Table Rendering Pipeline **File:** `/Users/anshumanbiswas/play/go-wiki/render/renderer.go` The rendering pipeline: 1. **Pre-processing** (line 119): `normalizeInlinePipeTables()` - Converts collapsed pipe tables to multi-line format 2. **Markdown rendering** (line 126): Uses `blackfriday.Run()` with these extensions: - `blackfriday.CommonExtensions` - `blackfriday.AutoHeadingIDs` - `blackfriday.FencedCode` - `blackfriday.Tables` - **Enables Markdown table support** - `blackfriday.Strikethrough` 3. **Post-processing**: Additional transformations for mermaid, task lists, YouTube embeds, etc. **File:** `/Users/anshumanbiswas/play/go-wiki/render/filters_pre.go` (lines 175-189) The `normalizeInlinePipeTables()` function: - Detects inline pipe tables in `

` tags that contain pipes - Checks if a paragraph has >=8 pipes or contains `---` (table delimiter) - Converts `| |` sequences to multi-line format for proper markdown parsing ### 4. CSS Styling for Tables **File:** `/Users/anshumanbiswas/play/taskai/web/src/index.css` Table styling uses **Tailwind CSS Typography plugin** prose classes: - Uses `prose prose-invert` classes on the preview container (line 832 in WikiEditor.tsx) - Tailwind Typography automatically styles tables with: - `--tw-prose-th-borders` - Table header borders - `--tw-prose-td-borders` - Table cell borders - Light mode overrides (lines 115-116 in index.css): ```css --tw-prose-th-borders: #d1d5db; --tw-prose-td-borders: #e5e7eb; ``` - Dark mode uses Tailwind's default `prose-invert` styling ### 5. go-wiki Package Table Capabilities **Repository:** `/Users/anshumanbiswas/play/go-wiki/` The go-wiki package provides: - **Core renderer:** Uses blackfriday v2 which supports GitHub-flavored markdown tables - **Table format supported:** ``` | Header 1 | Header 2 | |----------|----------| | Cell 1 | Cell 2 | ``` - **Pre-processing:** Intelligently normalizes tables that users paste as collapsed inline content - **No table-specific rendering options:** Table styling is handled by the HTML renderer and CSS (Tailwind) ### Key Rendering Path: 1. User writes markdown with table syntax in WikiEditor 2. Content sent to `/api/wiki/preview` endpoint 3. Backend pre-processes with `normalizeInlinePipeTables()` 4. Blackfriday renders markdown → HTML (with `blackfriday.Tables` flag enabled) 5. HTML returned to frontend 6. Rendered in `

` with `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` 7. Tailwind Typography prose classes style the ``, ``, ``, ``, `
`, `` elements All relevant file paths have been listed above with complete context for understanding the table rendering implementation.

1→package render
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"strings"
     5→
     6→	"github.com/russross/blackfriday/v2"
     7→)
     8→
     9→// RendererOptions toggles features without touching code.
    10→type RendererOptions struct {
    11→	AddListClasses       bool   // add classes to <ul>/<ol>/<li>
    12→	AddBlockquoteClasses bool   // style blockquotes
    13→	EnableLightbox       bool   // wrap images in anchors for lightbox
    14→	EnableYouTubeEmbeds  bool   // turn plain YT links into iframes
    15→	EnableTaskListHTML   bool   // turn - [x] into checkboxes
    16→	EnableMermaid        bool   // convert ```mermaid to <div class="mermaid">
    17→	ProtectInlineCSS     bool   // strip one-line CSS outsiders pasted accidentally
    18→	EnableDrawEmbeds     bool   // turn [draw:id] shortcodes into go-draw iframes
    19→	DrawBasePath         string // URL prefix for go-draw (e.g. "/draw"), required when EnableDrawEmbeds is true
    20→	EnableReferences     bool   // turn [^N] / [^N]: text into superscript citations with a reference list
    21→}
    22→
    23→// ClassConfig allows consumers to override the default CSS classes
    24→// applied by the list and blockquote post-processing filters.
    25→type ClassConfig struct {
    26→	ULClass         string // default: "list-disc pl-2"
    27→	OLClass         string // default: "list-decimal pl-2"
    28→	LIClass         string // default: "mb-2"
    29→	BlockquoteClass string // default: Tailwind blockquote classes
    30→}
    31→
    32→// DefaultClassConfig returns Tailwind-compatible classes used by the blog.
    33→func DefaultClassConfig() ClassConfig {
    34→	return ClassConfig{
    35→		ULClass:         "list-disc pl-2",
    36→		OLClass:         "list-decimal pl-2",
    37→		LIClass:         "mb-2",
    38→		BlockquoteClass: `p-4 my-4 border-s-4 border-gray-300 bg-gray-50 dark:border-gray-500 dark:bg-gray-800`,
    39→	}
    40→}
    41→
    42→// DefaultOptions returns sane defaults with all features enabled.
    43→func DefaultOptions() RendererOptions {
    44→	return RendererOptions{
    45→		AddListClasses:       true,
    46→		AddBlockquoteClasses: true,
    47→		EnableLightbox:       true,
    48→		EnableYouTubeEmbeds:  true,
    49→		EnableTaskListHTML:   true,
    50→		EnableMermaid:        true,
    51→		ProtectInlineCSS:     true,
    52→		EnableDrawEmbeds:     false, // opt-in; requires DrawBasePath
    53→		EnableReferences:     true,
    54→	}
    55→}
    56→
    57→// MinimalOptions returns options with all features disabled —
    58→// only the core markdown rendering with no post-processing.
    59→func MinimalOptions() RendererOptions {
    60→	return RendererOptions{}
    61→}
    62→
    63→// Renderer holds the configuration for the markdown rendering pipeline.
    64→type Renderer struct {
    65→	Opt     RendererOptions
    66→	Classes ClassConfig
    67→}
    68→
    69→// NewRenderer creates a Renderer with DefaultClassConfig.
    70→func NewRenderer(opt RendererOptions) *Renderer {
    71→	return &Renderer{Opt: opt, Classes: DefaultClassConfig()}
    72→}
    73→
    74→// NewRendererWithClasses creates a Renderer with custom CSS classes.
    75→func NewRendererWithClasses(opt RendererOptions, classes ClassConfig) *Renderer {
    76→	return &Renderer{Opt: opt, Classes: classes}
    77→}
    78→
    79→// Render runs the full pipeline and returns final HTML.
    80→func (r *Renderer) Render(content string) string {
    81→	html, _ := r.RenderWithDebug(content, false)
    82→	return html
    83→}
    84→
    85→// RenderWithDebug returns the final HTML and (optionally) every stage output for inspection.
    86→func (r *Renderer) RenderWithDebug(content string, includeStages bool) (string, map[string]string) {
    87→	stages := map[string]string{}
    88→
    89→	stage := func(name, s string) string {
    90→		if includeStages {
    91→			stages[name] = s
    92→		}
    93→		return s
    94→	}
    95→
    96→	// --- PRE ---
    97→	s := content
    98→	s = stage("00_raw", s)
    99→	s = normalizeWhitespaceAndBreaks(s)
   100→	s = stage("01_normalized", s)
   101→
   102→	if r.Opt.ProtectInlineCSS {
   103→		s = stripStyleSnippets(s)
   104→		s = stage("02_strip_style_snippets", s)
   105→	}
   106→
   107→	s = replaceMoreTag(s)
   108→	s = stage("03_more_tag", s)
   109→
   110→	// Convert fenced code blocks early so that subsequent preprocessing
   111→	// steps (heading conversion, list separation, etc.) do not modify
   112→	// content that is inside ``` fences.
   113→	s = convertFences(s)
   114→	s = stage("03b_fences", s)
   115→
   116→	s = unwrapListLikeContainers(s)
   117→	s = ensureListSeparation(s)
   118→	s = preprocessLooseMarkdownHTML(s)
   119→	s = normalizeInlinePipeTables(s)
   120→	if r.Opt.EnableReferences {
   121→		s = processReferences(s)
   122→	}
   123→	s = stage("04_preprocessed", s)
   124→
   125→	// --- MARKDOWN ---
   126→	md := renderMarkdown(s)
   127→	md = stage("05_markdown", md)
   128→
   129→	// --- POST ---
   130→	if r.Opt.EnableMermaid {
   131→		md = transformMermaidBlocks(md)
   132→		md = stage("06_mermaid", md)
   133→	}
   134→	if r.Opt.EnableTaskListHTML {
   135→		md = taskListToHTML(md)
   136→		md = stage("07_tasklist", md)
   137→	}
   138→	if r.Opt.EnableYouTubeEmbeds {
   139→		md = embedYouTube(md)
   140→		md = stage("08_youtube", md)
   141→	}
   142→	if r.Opt.EnableDrawEmbeds && r.Opt.DrawBasePath != "" {
   143→		md = embedDraw(md, r.Opt.DrawBasePath)
   144→		md = stage("08b_draw", md)
   145→	}
   146→	if r.Opt.AddListClasses {
   147→		md = addListClasses(md, r.Classes)
   148→		md = stage("09_list_classes", md)
   149→	}
   150→	if r.Opt.AddBlockquoteClasses {
   151→		md = addBlockquoteClasses(md, r.Classes)
   152→		md = stage("10_blockquote_classes", md)
   153→	}
   154→	md = convertInlineEmphasisInHTML(md)
   155→	md = stage("11_inline_emphasis", md)
   156→
   157→	if r.Opt.EnableLightbox {
   158→		md = wrapImageGalleries(md)
   159→		md = stage("12_lightbox", md)
   160→	}
   161→
   162→	final := md
   163→	return final, stages
   164→}
   165→
   166→// ---- Markdown renderer ----
   167→
   168→func renderMarkdown(content string) string {
   169→	if !strings.HasSuffix(content, "\n") {
   170→		content += "\n"
   171→	}
   172→	exts := blackfriday.CommonExtensions |
   173→		blackfriday.AutoHeadingIDs |
   174→		blackfriday.FencedCode |
   175→		blackfriday.Tables |
   176→		blackfriday.Strikethrough
   177→	renderer := blackfriday.NewHTMLRenderer(blackfriday.HTMLRendererParameters{})
   178→	out := blackfriday.Run([]byte(content), blackfriday.WithExtensions(exts), blackfriday.WithRenderer(renderer))
   179→	return string(out)
   180→}

{"page_id":54,"content":"# AskBenny — Infrastructure Reconnaissance

Last updated: 2026-03-31

---

## DNS & Network Analysis

### A Records (askbenny.ca)
- `3.170.19.71`, `3.170.19.57`, `3.170.19.21`, `3.170.19.47` — **AWS CloudFront** IPs

### AAAA Records
- None (no IPv6)

### NS Records
- `mario.ns.cloudflare.com`, `ali.ns.cloudflare.com`
- **Cloudflare DNS-only** (not proxying — traffic goes directly to CloudFront)

### MX Records
- `1 smtp.google.com` — **Google Workspace** for email

### TXT Records
- SPF: `include:341675126.spf07.hubspotemail.net` — **HubSpot** email sending
- Facebook domain verification
- Two Google site verifications

### www.askbenny.ca
- CNAME → `d2jalznjcvs1mc.cloudfront.net` — **S3 bucket redirect** (301 → `https://askbenny.ca/`)

### Key Finding
Cloudflare is used **only for DNS**. The actual CDN is **AWS CloudFront**. Origin IP is hidden behind CloudFront.

---

## HTTP & Header Fingerprinting (askbenny.ca)

| Header | Value | Inference |
|---|---|---|
| `x-powered-by` | `Next.js` | Next.js application |
| `x-opennext` | `1` | **OpenNext** adapter (Next.js deployed outside Vercel) |
| `x-amzn-requestid` | present | AWS origin (Lambda) |
| `x-amzn-trace-id` | present | AWS X-Ray tracing enabled |
| `x-cache` | `Miss from cloudfront` | AWS CloudFront CDN |
| `x-amz-cf-pop` | `IAD61-P9` | Served from us-east-1 (Virginia) |
| `x-clerk-auth-status` | `signed-out` | **Clerk** authentication |
| `x-clerk-auth-reason` | `session-token-and-uat-missing` | Clerk SSR middleware active |
| `cache-control` | `private, no-cache` | Dynamic SSR, not cached |

**Verdict:** Next.js running via OpenNext on AWS Lambda + CloudFront. NOT on Vercel.

---

## TLS / Certificate Transparency

### Current Certificate
- **Issuer:** Amazon RSA 2048 M01 (AWS ACM)
- **SANs:** `askbenny.ca`, `www.askbenny.us`, `askbenny.us`
- Validity: Dec 2025 – Jan 2027

### Historical Issuers (from crt.sh)
- Let's Encrypt, Google Trust Services, Cloudflare, GoDaddy, Amazon
- Suggests migration: GoDaddy → Cloudflare → AWS over time

---

## Subdomain Enumeration (from crt.sh)

| Subdomain | CNAME / Target | Platform |
|---|---|---|
| `api.askbenny.ca` | `d-72mqcg3i9e.execute-api.us-east-1.amazonaws.com` | **AWS API Gateway** |
| `api-dev.askbenny.ca` | `d-1b6xwp477k.execute-api.us-east-1.amazonaws.com` | **AWS API Gateway** (dev) |
| `agents.askbenny.ca` | `9leqg8sj.up.railway.app` | **Railway** |
| `agents-dev.askbenny.ca` | `mdclrnvn.up.railway.app` | **Railway** (dev) |
| `chat.askbenny.ca` | `llm-service-2xwv.onrender.com` | **Render** (GCP us-west1) |
| `docs.askbenny.ca` | `d63ss6zirl10g.cloudfront.net` | **AWS S3 + CloudFront** |
| `dev.askbenny.ca` | `d29d8v5uxka5wa.cloudfront.net` | **AWS CloudFront** (dev frontend) |
| `checkout.askbenny.ca` | `hosted-checkout.stripecdn.com` | **Stripe** hosted checkout |
| `mcp.askbenny.ca` | `cf3f39a7a257c775.vercel-dns-016.com` | **Vercel** |
| `mcp-dev.askbenny.ca` | `cf3f39a7a257c775.vercel-dns-016.com` | **Vercel** |
| `connect.askbenny.ca` | `conversationser-kvzmtbst-...us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com` | **AWS ELB** (ECS/EC2) |
| `connect-dev.askbenny.ca` | `conversationser-vuofuetz-...us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com` | **AWS ELB** (dev) |
| `oscar.askbenny.ca` | Cloudflare IPs (proxied) | **Heroku** (Laravel/PHP) |
| `clerk.askbenny.ca` | `frontend-api.clerk.services` | **Clerk** (managed) |
| `accounts.askbenny.ca` | `accounts.clerk.services` | **Clerk** (managed) |

### CDN Bypass Candidates
- `agents.askbenny.ca` → Railway (no CDN, direct)
- `chat.askbenny.ca` → Render (origin exposed in CNAME chain)
- `connect.askbenny.ca` → AWS ELB (direct, no CloudFront)

---

## Frontend Technology

- **Framework:** Next.js 14+ (App Router, React Server Components)
- **Deployment:** OpenNext on AWS Lambda (`x-opennext: 1`)
- **I18n:** 30+ languages via `I18nProvider`
- **Fonts:** League Spartan, Outfit (WOFF2)
- **Docs site:** Static HTML from S3 + CloudFront

---

## Backend / API Architecture

| Service | Endpoint | Technology | Hosting |
|---|---|---|---|
| REST API | `api.askbenny.ca` | AWS API Gateway → Lambda | AWS us-east-1 |
| Chat/LLM | `chat.askbenny.ca` | Node.js/Express (helmet headers) | Render (GCP us-west1) |
| Agents | `agents.askbenny.ca` | Unknown runtime | Railway |
| Connect | `connect.askbenny.ca` | ELB-backed service (ECS/EC2) | AWS us-east-1 |
| MCP Server | `mcp.askbenny.ca` | Vercel serverless | Vercel |
| Oscar | `oscar.askbenny.ca` | Laravel (PHP) | Heroku (via Cloudflare) |
| Auth | `clerk.askbenny.ca` | Clerk managed | Clerk SaaS |
| Payments | `checkout.askbenny.ca` | Stripe hosted checkout | Stripe SaaS |

### Oscar Evidence
- `laravel_session` cookie, `XSRF-TOKEN` cookie, `via: 2.0 heroku-router` header
- Likely a legacy internal tool

---

## Server Locations

| Service | Location | Country |
|---|---|---|
| Main site (CloudFront) | Virginia, US | 🇺🇸 USA |
| API Gateway | Ashburn, Virginia, US | 🇺🇸 USA |
| Connect (ELB) | Ashburn, Virginia, US | 🇺🇸 USA |
| Agents (Railway) | San Francisco, CA, US | 🇺🇸 USA |
| Chat/LLM (Render) | San Francisco, CA, US | 🇺🇸 USA |
| Oscar (Heroku) | San Francisco, CA, US | 🇺🇸 USA |
| MCP (Vercel) | Edge (global) | 🌐 Global |

Despite being a `.ca` domain, **all origin infrastructure is in the USA**.

---

## Third-Party Services

| Category | Service | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Analytics | Google Analytics (`G-K0M45WJKHT`) | Script tag |
| Tag Management | Google Tag Manager (`GTM-TKH5GLNP`) | Script tag |
| Marketing Pixel | Facebook Pixel (`1677316992881351`) | Script + noscript |
| Live Chat | Crisp (`937763c7-bf1f-4adc-8026-3485127d8b21`) | Widget script |
| CRM / Email | HubSpot (account `341675126`) | Script + SPF record |
| Auth | Clerk | Headers, subdomains, cookies |
| Payments | Stripe | Hosted checkout subdomain |
| Affiliate | Rewardful | `window._rwq` reference |
| AI/LLM | Custom service on Render | `chat.askbenny.ca` |

---

## Architecture Diagram

```
              Cloudflare (DNS only)
                     │
              AWS CloudFront (CDN + TLS via ACM)
                     │
              OpenNext (Next.js adapter)
                     │
              AWS Lambda (SSR + API routes)
                     │
              AWS API Gateway (api.askbenny.ca)
                     │
         ┌───────────┼───────────────┐
         │           │               │
    AWS ELB      Railway          Render
   (connect)    (agents)        (chat/LLM)
                                    │
                               GCP us-west1

  ┌──────────┬──────────┬──────────┐
  Clerk      Stripe     HubSpot    Vercel (MCP)
 (auth)    (payments)   (CRM)     (edge func)

  Heroku (oscar — Laravel/PHP internal tool)
```

---

## Confidence & Unknowns

### Confirmed (definitive)
- OpenNext on AWS Lambda + CloudFront — `x-opennext: 1`, `x-amzn-requestid`, ACM cert
- AWS API Gateway for REST API — CNAME is `execute-api.us-east-1.amazonaws.com`
- Railway for agents — CNAME + Railway headers
- Render for chat — CNAME + `x-render-origin-server` header
- Vercel for MCP — `server: Vercel`, `x-vercel-cache` headers
- Heroku for Oscar — `laravel_session`, `via: 2.0 heroku-router`
- Clerk, Stripe, HubSpot — multiple corroborating signals

### Cannot Be Determined
- Exact Lambda runtime (Node.js likely, but unconfirmed)
- Database layer (likely RDS or DynamoDB)
- IaC tooling (SST, CDK, Terraform, etc.)
- Origin IPs behind CloudFront
- AI model provider behind `chat.askbenny.ca`
- Internal service communication patterns

### Observations
- Multi-platform approach (AWS + Railway + Render + Vercel + Heroku) suggests rapid iteration
- Laravel on Heroku (`oscar`) likely predates the AWS migration
- Proper dev/staging environments with `-dev` subdomains mirroring production

| `agents-dev.askbenny.ca` | `mdclrnvn.up.railway.app` | **Railway** (dev) |
| `chat.askbenny.ca` | `llm-service-2xwv.onrender.com` | **Render** (GCP us-west1) |
| `docs.askbenny.ca` | `d63ss6zirl10g.cloudfront.net` | **AWS S3 + CloudFront** |
| `dev.askbenny.ca` | `d29d8v5uxka5wa.cloudfront.net` | **AWS CloudFront** (dev frontend) |
| `checkout.askbenny.ca` | `hosted-checkout.stripecdn.com` | **Stripe** hosted checkout |
| `mcp.askbenny.ca` | `cf3f39a7a257c775.vercel-dns-016.com` | **Vercel** |
| `mcp-dev.askbenny.ca` | `cf3f39a7a257c775.vercel-dns-016.com` | **Vercel** |
| `connect.askbenny.ca` | `conversationser-kvzmtbst-...us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com` | **AWS ELB** (ECS/EC2) |
| `connect-dev.askbenny.ca` | `conversationser-vuofuetz-...us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com` | **AWS ELB** (dev) |
| `oscar.askbenny.ca` | Cloudflare IPs (proxied) | **Heroku** (Laravel/PHP) |
| `clerk.askbenny.ca` | `frontend-api.clerk.services` | **Clerk** (managed) |
| `accounts.askbenny.ca` | `accounts.clerk.services` | **Clerk** (managed) |

### CDN Bypass Candidates
- `agents.askbenny.ca` → Railway (no CDN, direct)
- `chat.askbenny.ca` → Render (origin exposed in CNAME chain)
- `connect.askbenny.ca` → AWS ELB (direct, no CloudFront)

---

## Frontend Technology

- **Framework:** Next.js 14+ (App Router, React Server Components)
- **Deployment:** OpenNext on AWS Lambda (`x-opennext: 1`)
- **I18n:** 30+ languages via `I18nProvider`
- **Fonts:** League Spartan, Outfit (WOFF2)
- **Docs site:** Static HTML from S3 + CloudFront

---

## Backend / API Architecture

| Service | Endpoint | Technology | Hosting |
|---|---|---|---|
| REST API | `api.askbenny.ca` | AWS API Gateway → Lambda | AWS us-east-1 |
| Chat/LLM | `chat.askbenny.ca` | Node.js/Express (helmet headers) | Render (GCP us-west1) |
| Agents | `agents.askbenny.ca` | Unknown runtime | Railway |
| Connect | `connect.askbenny.ca` | ELB-backed service (ECS/EC2) | AWS us-east-1 |
| MCP Server | `mcp.askbenny.ca` | Vercel serverless | Vercel |
| Oscar | `oscar.askbenny.ca` | Laravel (PHP) | Heroku (via Cloudflare) |
| Auth | `clerk.askbenny.ca` | Clerk managed | Clerk SaaS |
| Payments | `checkout.askbenny.ca` | Stripe hosted checkout | Stripe SaaS |

### Oscar Evidence
- `laravel_session` cookie, `XSRF-TOKEN` cookie, `via: 2.0 heroku-router` header
- Likely a legacy internal tool

---

## Server Locations

| Service | Location | Country |
|---|---|---|
| Main site (CloudFront) | Virginia, US | 🇺🇸 USA |
| API Gateway | Ashburn, Virginia, US | 🇺🇸 USA |
| Connect (ELB) | Ashburn, Virginia, US | 🇺🇸 USA |
| Agents (Railway) | San Francisco, CA, US | 🇺🇸 USA |
| Chat/LLM (Render) | San Francisco, CA, US | 🇺🇸 USA |
| Oscar (Heroku) | San Francisco, CA, US | 🇺🇸 USA |
| MCP (Vercel) | Edge (global) | 🌐 Global |

Despite being a `.ca` domain, **all origin infrastructure is in the USA**.

---

## Third-Party Services

| Category | Service | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Analytics | Google Analytics (`G-K0M45WJKHT`) | Script tag |
| Tag Management | Google Tag Manager (`GTM-TKH5GLNP`) | Script tag |
| Marketing Pixel | Facebook Pixel (`1677316992881351`) | Script + noscript |
| Live Chat | Crisp (`937763c7-bf1f-4adc-8026-3485127d8b21`) | Widget script |
| CRM / Email | HubSpot (account `341675126`) | Script + SPF record |
| Auth | Clerk | Headers, subdomains, cookies |
| Payments | Stripe | Hosted checkout subdomain |
| Affiliate | Rewardful | `window._rwq` reference |
| AI/LLM | Custom service on Render | `chat.askbenny.ca` |

---

## Architecture Diagram

```
              Cloudflare (DNS only)
                     │
              AWS CloudFront (CDN + TLS via ACM)
                     │
              OpenNext (Next.js adapter)
                     │
              AWS Lambda (SSR + API routes)
                     │
              AWS API Gateway (api.askbenny.ca)
                     │
         ┌───────────┼───────────────┐
         │           │               │
    AWS ELB      Railway          Render
   (connect)    (agents)        (chat/LLM)
                                    │
                               GCP us-west1

  ┌──────────┬──────────┬──────────┐
  Clerk      Stripe     HubSpot    Vercel (MCP)
 (auth)    (payments)   (CRM)     (edge func)

  Heroku (oscar — Laravel/PHP internal tool)
```

---

## Confidence & Unknowns

### Confirmed (definitive)
- OpenNext on AWS Lambda + CloudFront — `x-opennext: 1`, `x-amzn-requestid`, ACM cert
- AWS API Gateway for REST API — CNAME is `execute-api.us-east-1.amazonaws.com`
- Railway for agents — CNAME + Railway headers
- Render for chat — CNAME + `x-render-origin-server` header
- Vercel for MCP — `server: Vercel`, `x-vercel-cache` headers
- Heroku for Oscar — `laravel_session`, `via: 2.0 heroku-router`
- Clerk, Stripe, HubSpot — multiple corroborating signals

### Cannot Be Determined
- Exact Lambda runtime (Node.js likely, but unconfirmed)
- Database layer (likely RDS or DynamoDB)
- IaC tooling (SST, CDK, Terraform, etc.)
- Origin IPs behind CloudFront
- AI model provider behind `chat.askbenny.ca`
- Internal service communication patterns

### Observations
- Multi-platform approach (AWS + Railway + Render + Vercel + Heroku) suggests rapid iteration
- Laravel on Heroku (`oscar`) likely predates the AWS migration
- Proper dev/staging environments with `-dev` subdomains mirroring production

package main

import (
	"fmt"
	gowiki "github.com/anchoo2kewl/go-wiki"
	"github.com/anchoo2kewl/go-wiki/render"
)

func main() {
	wiki := gowiki.New(
		gowiki.WithClassConfig(render.ClassConfig{
			ULClass:         "list-disc pl-6 space-y-1",
			OLClass:         "list-decimal pl-6 space-y-1",
			LIClass:         "",
			BlockquoteClass: "border-l-4 border-blue-500/40 pl-4 italic text-gray-300",
		}),
		gowiki.WithDrawBasePath("/draw"),
	)

	content := `| Header | Value | Inference |
|---|---|---|
| ` + "`x-powered-by`" + ` | ` + "`Next.js`" + ` | Next.js application |
| ` + "`x-opennext`" + ` | ` + "`1`" + ` | **OpenNext** adapter |
`

	html := wiki.RenderContent(content)
	fmt.Println(html)
}
EOF

content.content:
# Analysis of Provided Web Content

Based on the content supplied, I can only observe:

**Limited HTML Structure**: The snippet shows only a `<title>` tag ("TaskAI - AI-Native Project Management") and a Mermaid.js initialization script block. 

**No Tables Present**: There is no table markup (HTML `<table>` elements) or pipe-formatted table syntax visible in the provided excerpt.

Everything builds. Here's a summary of the changes:

**Issue 1 - Agent attribution on wiki pages:**
- **Backend** (`wiki_handlers.go`): Added `AgentName` field to `WikiPageResponse`. For single page fetch, queries `agent_name` via raw SQL. For list, bulk-fetches all agent names for the project.
- **Frontend** (`api.ts`): Added `agent_name` to `WikiPage` interface.
- **Frontend** (`WikiEditor.tsx`): Updated "Last edited by" section to show `"Claude Code for Anshuman Biswas"` format with an AI badge - matching the exact pattern used in task comments.

**Issue 2 - Table rendering:**
- Tables render correctly through go-wiki (confirmed with test) - no go-wiki changes needed.
- **CSS** (`index.css`): Added explicit table styles for both dark and light mode:
  - Visible borders (dark mode borders were too subtle with Tailwind defaults)
  - Proper padding on th/td
  - Header background tint
  - Hover highlighting on rows
  - Horizontal scroll for wide tables (`display: block; overflow-x: auto`)
  - Thicker header border separator

content.content: Launching skill: deploy-prod

content.text:
Promote TaskAI all the way to production: staging → UAT → production.

## Usage
```
/deploy-prod   # promotes current staging through UAT and into production
```

No arguments needed. This command always promotes from staging → UAT → production in sequence.

## Rules
- Staging must already be deployed and healthy before running this
- UAT is promoted from staging (main branch), never developed on directly
- Production is promoted after UAT succeeds
- Never manually SSH to servers — always use GitHub workflows
- If any step fails, stop and report — do not proceed to the next environment

## Steps

### 1. Verify staging is healthy
Run:
```bash
gh run list --workflow="Deploy to Staging" --limit=1 --json status,conclusion,updatedAt
```
If the last staging run is not `conclusion: success`, abort and tell the user to fix staging first.

### 2. Promote staging → UAT
```bash
./script/server promote staging uat
```
Then wait for the UAT workflow to complete:
```bash
gh run list --workflow="Deploy to UAT" --limit=1 --json databaseId,status,conclusion,updatedAt
```
Poll every 15 seconds (up to 5 minutes) until `status` is `completed`. If `conclusion` is not `success`, abort and report the failure with the run URL.

### 3. Promote staging → Production
```bash
./script/server promote staging prod
```
Then wait for the production workflow to complete:
```bash
gh run list --workflow="Deploy to Production" --limit=1 --json databaseId,status,conclusion,updatedAt
```
Poll every 15 seconds (up to 5 minutes) until `status` is `completed`.

### 4. Report final status
Show a summary table:

| Environment | Action | Status | Run |
|-------------|--------|--------|-----|
| Staging     | Source (no change) | ✅ success | #XXXXXXXXX |
| UAT         | Promoted from staging | ✅ success | #XXXXXXXXX |
| Production  | Promoted from staging | ✅ success | #XXXXXXXXX |

URLs:
- Staging:    https://staging.taskai.cc
- UAT:        https://uat.taskai.cc
- Production: https://taskai.cc

Monitor all runs: https://github.com/anchoo2kewl/taskai/actions


ARGUMENTS: fix(web): wiki agent attribution + table styling

- Add agent_name to wiki page API response for LLM attribution
- Show "Claude Code for User Name" with AI badge on wiki pages (matching comment format)
- Add explicit table CSS for dark/light mode: visible borders, padding, header bg, row hover, horizontal scroll
The user wants to skip UAT and go staging → prod directly. Let me first commit, push, verify staging, then promote to prod.

#!/bin/bash

# TaskAI Server Management Script
# Manages deployment, testing, health checks, and operations

set -e

# Colors for output
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
PURPLE='\033[0;35m'
CYAN='\033[0;36m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

# Server connections
PROD_HOST="31.97.102.48"
STAGING_HOST="129.213.82.37"
REMOTE_USER="ubuntu"

# Staging environment (129.213.82.37)
STAGING_DEPLOY_PATH="/home/ubuntu/taskai-staging"
STAGING_DOMAIN="staging.taskai.cc"
STAGING_API_PORT=8082
STAGING_WEB_PORT=8086
STAGING_MCP_PORT=8084
STAGING_MCP_DOMAIN="mcp.staging.taskai.cc"

# Production environment (31.97.102.48)
PROD_DEPLOY_PATH="/home/ubuntu/taskai"
PROD_DOMAIN="taskai.cc"
PROD_API_PORT=8081
PROD_WEB_PORT=8085
PROD_MCP_PORT=8083
PROD_MCP_DOMAIN="mcp.taskai.cc"

# Local development settings
LOCAL_WEB_PORT=8084
LOCAL_API_PORT=8083

# Resolve environment — sets REMOTE_HOST, REMOTE_SERVER, REMOTE_DEPLOY_PATH, etc.
resolve_env() {
    local env="${1:-staging}"
    case "$env" in
        prod|production)
            REMOTE_HOST="$PROD_HOST"
            REMOTE_DEPLOY_PATH="$PROD_DEPLOY_PATH"
            REMOTE_DOMAIN="$PROD_DOMAIN"
            REMOTE_API_PORT="$PROD_API_PORT"

    echo "  prod version         Show production version info"

    echo "  $0 promote                            # Promote staging → production"
    echo "  $0 promote staging uat                # Promote staging → UAT (when needed)"
    echo "  $0 promote uat prod                   # Promote UAT → production"
    echo "  $0 staging health                      # Check staging health"
    echo "  $0 prod health                         # Check production health"
    echo "  $0 staging logs api                    # View staging API logs"
    echo "  $0 db-migrate staging prod             # Copy staging DB to production"
    echo "  $0 db-migrate prod staging             # Copy production DB to staging"
}

# Deploy to staging (commit, push to main — Travis CI deploys automatically)
deploy_to_staging() {
    local commit_message="${1:-Deploy: automated deployment}"

    print_header "Deploying to Staging"

    # Check for uncommitted changes
    if [ -n "$(git status --porcelain)" ]; then
        print_status "Uncommitted changes detected, committing..."
        git add -A
        git commit -m "$commit_message

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>" || {
            print_error "Failed to commit changes"
            return 1
        }
    else
        print_status "No uncommitted changes found"
    fi

    # Push to remote — Travis CI picks up main branch pushes automatically
    print_status "Pushing to main (triggers Travis CI: tests + staging deploy)..."
    git push origin main || {
        print_error "Failed to push"
        return 1
    }

    print_success "Code pushed — Travis CI staging deployment triggered"
    print_status "Monitor: https://app.travis-ci.com/github/anchoo2kewl/taskai"
    echo ""

    # Wait for Travis to build + deploy (~5 min for fast deploy)
    print_status "Waiting for staging deployment (~5 minutes)..."
    sleep 120

    resolve_env staging
    print_status "Verifying staging deployment..."
    local max_attempts=40
    local attempt=1

    while [ $attempt -le $max_attempts ]; do
        if curl -f -s "https://$STAGING_DOMAIN/api/health" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            # Check if the deployed version matches our commit
            local deployed_commit=$(curl -s "https://$STAGING_DOMAIN/api/version" 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.backend.git_commit // empty')
            local local_commit=$(git rev-parse --short HEAD)

            if [[ -n "$deployed_commit" && "$deployed_commit" == "$local_commit"* ]]; then
                print_success "Staging deployment successful! (commit: $deployed_commit)"
            else
                print_success "Staging is healthy (deployed: ${deployed_commit:-unknown}, pushed: $local_commit)"
            fi
            echo ""
            echo -e "${CYAN}Staging URL:${NC} https://$STAGING_DOMAIN"
            echo -e "${CYAN}API Health:${NC} https://$STAGING_DOMAIN/api/health"
            echo ""

            # Show version information
            env_version staging
            echo ""

            print_status "To promote to production: $0 promote staging prod"
            return 0
        fi

        print_status "Waiting for staging to be ready (attempt $attempt/$max_attempts)..."
        sleep 5
        attempt=$((attempt + 1))
    done

    print_warning "Staging not responding yet — Travis build may still be running"
    print_status "Check Travis: https://app.travis-ci.com/github/anchoo2kewl/taskai"
    print_status "Check health: $0 staging health"
    return 1
}

# Promote between environments — Travis CI deploys on branch push
# Default: staging → prod (skip UAT). Use explicit args for UAT.
# Usage: promote [source] [destination]
#   promote                    → staging → prod (default)
#   promote staging prod       → staging → prod
#   promote staging uat        → staging → uat
#   promote uat prod           → uat → prod
promote() {
    local src="${1:-staging}"
    local dst="${2:-prod}"

    # Normalize aliases
    case "$src" in
        staging|stage) src="staging" ;;
        uat)           src="uat" ;;
        prod|production) src="prod" ;;
        *)
            print_error "Unknown source environment: '$src'"
            echo "  Usage: $0 promote [staging|uat] [uat|prod]"
            return 1
            ;;
    esac
    case "$dst" in
        uat)             dst="uat" ;;
        prod|production) dst="prod" ;;
        *)
            print_error "Unknown destination environment: '$dst'"
            echo "  Usage: $0 promote [staging|uat] [uat|prod]"
            return 1
